concepts.title: Witness
concepts.definition: Interpretation depends on the observer, the archive, and the surrounding context — not on the image alone.

concepts.title: Description vs Inference
concepts.definition: Description reports what is visibly present. Inference derives what is not directly visible. These must be kept separate.

concepts.title: Recurrence
concepts.definition: Recurrence is the detection of the same or structurally similar form appearing across multiple images, instances, or contexts.

concepts.title: Symbolic Candidate
concepts.definition: A recurring form that may carry compressed meaning — but whose symbolic status remains provisional pending contextual judgment.

concepts.title: Archive Context
concepts.definition: The dataset, curation history, institutional framing, and selection biases that shape what a machine reading system encounters and how it responds.

concepts.title: Interpretive Restraint
concepts.definition: The practice of assigning significance cautiously, explicitly, and only with stated context.

concepts.title: Prohibited Inferences
concepts.definition: Seven hard constraints on inferences agents must not make.
